perturbation.original_text: The Operating Company shall reimburse EmployeeCo for all direct or indirect costs and expenses incurred by EmployeeCo in connection with performing its obligations under this Agreement including, but not limited to: (a) salaries of the Services Employees; (b) the cost of employee benefits for the Services Employees, including 401(k), pension, bonuses and health insurance benefits (whether through insurance policies provided by third-parties or self-insurance); (c) costs associated with workers' compensation claims and other disputes or liabilities associated with the Services Employees; (d) severance costs with respect to any terminated Services Employees; and (e) all sales, use, employment, excise, value added or similar taxes, if any, that may be applicable from time to time with respect to the Services. Such reimbursements shall be made by the Operating Company to EmployeeCo in advance or immediately upon such costs being incurred, or otherwise in accordance with historical practice, unless otherwise agreed between the Operating Company and EmployeeCo.
perturbation.changed_text: The Operating Company shall reimburse EmployeeCo for all direct or indirect costs and expenses incurred by EmployeeCo in connection with performing its obligations under this Agreement including, but not limited to: (a) salaries of the Services Employees; (b) the cost of employee benefits for the Services Employees, including 401(k), pension, bonuses and health insurance benefits (whether through insurance policies provided by third-parties or self-insurance); (c) costs associated with workers' compensation claims and other disputes or liabilities associated with the Services Employees; (d) severance costs with respect to any terminated Services Employees, only if approved by the Operating Company; and (e) all sales, use, employment, excise, value added or similar taxes, if any, that may be applicable from time to time with respect to the Services. Such reimbursements shall be made by the Operating Company to EmployeeCo only after approval from the Operating Company for all costs being incurred, or otherwise in accordance with historical practice, unless otherwise agreed between the Operating Company and EmployeeCo.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty regarding the reimbursement of costs, particularly severance costs. Originally, the Operating Company *shall* reimburse EmployeeCo for all listed costs, including severance. The added condition 'only if approved by the Operating Company' for severance and making 'reimbursements only after approval' gives the Operating Company discretion, contradicting the initial mandatory reimbursement obligation. This could lead to disputes over what costs are actually reimbursed, especially since approval from Operating company for costs and reimbursements were not originally required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Operating Company shall reimburse EmployeeCo for all direct or indirect costs and expenses incurred by EmployeeCo in connection with performing its obligations under this Agreement including, but not limited to: (a) salaries of the Services Employees; (b) the cost of employee benefits for the Services Employees, including 401(k), pension, bonuses and health insurance benefits (whether through insurance policies provided by third-parties or self-insurance); (c) costs associated with workers' compensation claims and other disputes or liabilities associated with the Services Employees; (d) severance costs with respect to any terminated Services Employees, only if approved by the Operating Company; and (e) all sales, use, employment, excise, value added or similar taxes, if any, that may be applicable from time to time with respect to the Services. Such reimbursements shall be made by the Operating Company to EmployeeCo only after approval from the Operating Company for all costs being incurred, or otherwise in accordance with historical practice, unless otherwise agreed between the Operating Company and EmployeeCo.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Operating Company 'shall' reimburse EmployeeCo for all listed costs, including severance costs. The changed text adds the condition 'only if approved by the Operating Company' for severance costs and 'only after approval from the Operating Company for all costs'. This introduces a discretionary element, contradicting the initial mandatory reimbursement obligation. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting that the changed text introduces a direct contradiction with its initial provision that reimbursement 'shall' occur.
